name: feature-development
description: Develop code, then fan out to automated review and tests. On failure, loop back to developer with feedback. On success, create and watch a PR.

adopt-pr.name: Adopt existing PR (optional)
adopt-pr.description: If the plan text contains '<!-- adopt-pr: N -->', swap this worktree to PR N's head branch so downstream steps (developer, reviewer, test, pr-create) operate on the right branch and pr-create's existing 'PR already exists' path adopts the real PR. Otherwise no-op.
adopt-pr.prompt: Adopt an existing PR if the plan opts in.

on_failure.message: adopt-pr failed; continuing on the fresh workflow branch. If the plan intended to update an existing PR, the developer should manually `git fetch origin <branch> && git checkout -B <branch> origin/<branch>` before making changes.

developer.name: Developer Agent
developer.description: Write or revise code based on user instructions or feedback from reviewers/tests.

summary.description: Summary of changes made in this iteration

reviewer.name: Reviewer Agent
reviewer.description: Review code changes for correctness, style, and design.

on_failure.message:
The code review found issues that need to be fixed:

${steps.reviewer.output.review}

review.description: Review comments explaining what needs to change and why

approved.description: true if the code is ready to merge

on_failure.message:
Tests failed. Here is the test output:

${steps.test.output.results}

results.description: Full test output including failure messages and stack traces

pr-create.name: PR Creator
pr-create.description: Push the branch and create a pull request with an AI-generated title and summary.

pr_url.description: URL of the created pull request

pr-watch.name: PR Watcher
pr-watch.description: Watch the PR for merge, CI failures, or review comments.

on_failure.message:
The PR needs attention. GitHub feedback:

${steps.pr-watch.output.feedback}. Fix the issue with one or more new commits (rebasing onto origin/main is fine if there's a conflict). Do not push — downstream steps (test, reviewer, pr-creator) will re-run and pr-creator will update the existing PR.

feedback.description: CI failure details or reviewer comments from GitHub

git-pull.name: Update local repo
git-pull.description: Pull latest changes into the user's working tree after PR merge.
